name: AI Search
description: Adds an AI search bar to the Jellyfin web client. Ask in natural language and get movies from your own library, personalized to your watch history, backed by a self-hosted AI platform or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
overview: AI natural-language search & recommendations from your own library.

versions.changelog: History rows can now be deleted individually: a subtle cross fades in on hover (desktop) or is revealed by swiping (mobile), with a full swipe deleting immediately. Surprise me now morphs into a full-width button revealing Movies/TV Shows and Personalize to my taste toggles before running, instead of firing immediately.

Fix: model list dropdown could come back empty even after saving the endpoint URL/key. The Models endpoint now normalizes different provider response shapes (bare array, {data:[...]}, {models:[...]}) and surfaces the real HTTP error in the config page instead of silently showing 'No models returned'.

People queries now work: the model is given each candidate's cast (actors with characters, plus directors) instead of guessing from memory, and only genuine matches are returned. Built against Jellyfin 10.11.0 for 10.11.x compatibility.

Direct mode now builds a local semantic index (embeddings + in-memory nearest-neighbour search) over any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, so results match by meaning instead of sending a catalog slice. Nightly refresh via a scheduled task. Built against Jellyfin 10.11.0 for 10.11.x compatibility.
